skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem:
Five robins each have a nest. The table shows an original data set of the number of eggs that each robin laid in its nest.

Nest12345
Eggs891099

A sixth nest with 3 eggs is added to create a new data set. Which of the following correctly compares the means of the two data sets?

answerOptions.content: The means of both data sets are equal.
The mean of the original data set is less than the mean of the new data set.
There is not enough information to compare the means.
The mean of the original data set is greater than the mean of the new data set.

rationale:
Choice D is correct. The mean of the original data set is (8+9+10+9+9) ÷ 5 = 45 ÷ 5 = 9. The new data set adds a value of 3, so its mean is (45+3) ÷ 6 = 48 ÷ 6 = 8. Since 9 > 8, the original mean is greater than the new mean.

Choice A is incorrect. The means are not equal: the original mean is 9 and the new mean is 8.

Choice B is incorrect. Adding a value (3) below the original mean lowers the mean, so the original mean is greater, not less.

Choice C is incorrect. All needed values are given, so the means can be calculated and compared.
